description_long: This is a vintage biplane with two stacked wings, each featuring roundels in blue, white, and red, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is painted in a yellow and black checkerboard pattern with a rounded nose and a wooden propeller at the front. The tail section is adorned with a tricolor vertical stabilizer in red, white, and blue, and the horizontal stabilizers are tan with a slight dihedral angle. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and a single seat visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall construction appears to be primarily wood and fabric, with some metal components for structural support and the engine housing.
description: A vintage biplane with tan wings, checkerboard fuselage, wooden propeller, and open cockpit, featuring colorful roundels and a tricolor tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual tan wings with roundels, wooden propeller, and the engine cowling.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the checkerboard fuselage, open cockpit, and tricolor tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tricolor vertical stabilizer, tail skid, and the symmetrical wing structure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit opening, checkerboard pattern, and the propeller profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets; slight dihedral angle on lower wing.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a small windshield.
